techStack.frontend: Next.js 14 App Router, React 18 (Server & Client Components), TailwindCSS 3.4, TypeScript 5.3
techStack.backend: Next.js API Routes, Prisma ORM 5.9, NextAuth.js 4.24
techStack.auth: NextAuth.js with JWT sessions and credentials provider
techStack.stateManagement: TanStack Query 5.20 for server state, React hooks for local state

strengths: Modern Next.js 14 App Router with Server Components for optimal performance
Type-safe database access with Prisma ORM and TypeScript
Proper separation of concerns with /app, /components, /lib structure
React Query for efficient server state management and caching
Secure authentication with NextAuth.js and password hashing
Scalable file storage with AWS S3
Stripe integration for reliable payment processing
Enum-based type safety for status fields (VideoStatus, FeedbackStatus, etc.)
weaknesses: No caching layer (Redis) for frequently accessed data like trainer listings
Missing API versioning strategy - breaking changes will affect all clients
No service layer or repository pattern - business logic mixed with route handlers
Database queries not optimized - potential N+1 queries, missing indexes on filtered fields
No input validation framework implemented - Zod installed but not used consistently
Missing error tracking and monitoring infrastructure (no Sentry/APM)
No rate limiting on any endpoints - vulnerable to abuse
Prisma client created without connection pooling optimization
No separation between public and authenticated API routes
Email and S3 clients recreated on each use instead of singleton pattern
scalabilityConcerns: No pagination on list endpoints - will fail with large datasets
Missing database indexes on frequently queried fields (createdAt, status, userId combinations)
No caching strategy - every request hits database
No queue system for background jobs (video processing, email sending, webhook retries)
No CDN configured for static assets and video thumbnails
Missing horizontal scaling considerations - no session management for multi-instance deployments
No database read replicas strategy for separating read/write loads
File upload uses presigned URLs but no cleanup for abandoned uploads - storage bloat
No video processing or compression - large files will consume bandwidth

databaseSchemaChanges.changes: Add fields for email verification tokens, password reset tokens, notification preferences, and last password change tracking
databaseSchemaChanges.migration: ALTER TABLE "User" ADD COLUMN "verificationToken" TEXT, ADD COLUMN "verificationTokenExpiry" TIMESTAMP, ADD COLUMN "resetToken" TEXT, ADD COLUMN "resetTokenExpiry" TIMESTAMP, ADD COLUMN "preferences" JSONB DEFAULT '{}', ADD COLUMN "lastPasswordChange" TIMESTAMP; CREATE INDEX "User_verificationToken_idx" ON "User"("verificationToken"); CREATE INDEX "User_resetToken_idx" ON "User"("resetToken");
databaseSchemaChanges.reasoning: Email verification and password reset are critical security features. Preferences JSON field allows flexible storage of user settings without schema changes. Indexes on tokens enable fast lookup during verification.

databaseSchemaChanges.changes: Add indexes on (userId, createdAt) composite and (userId, status) for efficient querying, add processingMetadata JSON field for future video processing info
databaseSchemaChanges.migration: CREATE INDEX "Video_userId_createdAt_idx" ON "Video"("userId", "createdAt" DESC); CREATE INDEX "Video_userId_status_idx" ON "Video"("userId", "status"); ALTER TABLE "Video" ADD COLUMN "processingMetadata" JSONB;
databaseSchemaChanges.reasoning: Composite indexes dramatically improve dashboard query performance when fetching user's videos ordered by date. processingMetadata field allows storage of thumbnail URLs, quality versions, processing status without schema changes.

databaseSchemaChanges.changes: Add indexes on (videoId, status) and (trainerId, status, createdAt) for trainer dashboard queries
databaseSchemaChanges.migration: CREATE INDEX "Feedback_videoId_status_idx" ON "Feedback"("videoId", "status"); CREATE INDEX "Feedback_trainerId_status_createdAt_idx" ON "Feedback"("trainerId", "status", "createdAt" DESC);
databaseSchemaChanges.reasoning: Trainers need to efficiently query pending feedback assignments and historical completed work. These composite indexes prevent full table scans.

databaseSchemaChanges.changes: Add availability, maxDailyReviews, and responseTime tracking fields
databaseSchemaChanges.migration: ALTER TABLE "Trainer" ADD COLUMN "availableHours" JSONB DEFAULT '{}', ADD COLUMN "maxDailyReviews" INTEGER DEFAULT 10, ADD COLUMN "avgResponseTimeHours" FLOAT; CREATE INDEX "Trainer_rating_totalReviews_idx" ON "Trainer"("rating" DESC, "totalReviews" DESC);
databaseSchemaChanges.reasoning: Availability management prevents trainer burnout. Response time tracking helps users set expectations. Composite index on rating and reviews improves trainer listing sorting.

databaseSchemaChanges.changes: Add index on (feedbackId, timestamp) for efficient retrieval of timestamped comments in chronological order
databaseSchemaChanges.reasoning: Timestamped comments are displayed in video timeline order. This index ensures fast retrieval for video player UI.

databaseSchemaChanges.changes: Add metadata JSON field for storing package details, Stripe session info, and refund reasons
databaseSchemaChanges.migration: ALTER TABLE "Transaction" ADD COLUMN "metadata" JSONB DEFAULT '{}';
databaseSchemaChanges.reasoning: Flexible metadata storage enables tracking purchase packages, promotional codes, refund reasons without schema changes. Useful for analytics and customer support.

databaseSchemaChanges.changes: Restructure to track credit history as ledger instead of single balance row. Add creditChange and reason fields.
databaseSchemaChanges.migration: CREATE TABLE "CreditLedger" ("id" TEXT PRIMARY KEY, "userId" TEXT NOT NULL, "creditChange" INTEGER NOT NULL, "balance" INTEGER NOT NULL, "reason" TEXT NOT NULL, "transactionId" TEXT, "createdAt" TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW()); CREATE INDEX "CreditLedger_userId_createdAt_idx" ON "CreditLedger"("userId", "createdAt" DESC); ALTER TABLE "Credit" DROP CONSTRAINT IF EXISTS "Credit_transactionId_key";
databaseSchemaChanges.reasoning: Ledger-based credit tracking provides full audit trail of credit changes (purchases, usage, refunds). Enables debugging credit discrepancies and provides transparency to users. Current design only tracks balance without history.

databaseSchemaChanges.schema:
model StripeWebhookLog {
  id              String   @id @default(cuid())
  eventId         String   @unique
  eventType       String
  status          String   @default("pending")
  payload         Json
  error           String?
  attempts        Int      @default(1)
  createdAt       DateTime @default(now())
  processedAt     DateTime?
  
  @@index([eventId])
  @@index([status])
  @@index([eventType])
}
databaseSchemaChanges.reasoning: Idempotency enforcement and audit trail for Stripe webhooks. Prevents duplicate credit allocations if Stripe resends events. Error tracking enables debugging payment issues. Critical for financial integrity.

databaseSchemaChanges.schema:
model Notification {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId    String
  type      String
  title     String
  message   String
  link      String?
  read      Boolean  @default(false)
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  
  user User @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  
  @@index([userId, read, createdAt])
  @@index([userId, createdAt])
}
databaseSchemaChanges.reasoning: Real-time notifications improve engagement. Database persistence ensures notifications survive across sessions and devices. Composite indexes enable efficient querying of unread notifications.

databaseSchemaChanges.schema:
model TrainerReview {
  id         String   @id @default(cuid())
  trainerId  String
  userId     String
  feedbackId String   @unique
  rating     Int
  comment    String?
  isVerified Boolean  @default(true)
  createdAt  DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt  DateTime @updatedAt
  
  trainer  Trainer  @relation(fields: [trainerId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  user     User     @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  feedback Feedback @relation(fields: [feedbackId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  
  @@index([trainerId, createdAt])
  @@index([userId])
  @@unique([userId, feedbackId])
}
databaseSchemaChanges.reasoning: Trainer rating system requires separate model to track reviews. One review per feedback ensures only users who received service can rate. Verified badge indicates review is from actual customer.

databaseSchemaChanges.schema:
model Purchase {
  id                   String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId               String
  stripeSessionId      String   @unique
  stripePaymentIntentId String?
  amount               Int
  credits              Int
  status               String   @default("pending")
  metadata             Json     @default("{}")
  createdAt            DateTime @default(now())
  completedAt          DateTime?
  
  user User @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  
  @@index([userId, createdAt])
  @@index([status])
}
databaseSchemaChanges.reasoning: Dedicated purchase tracking separate from generic Transaction model. Links Stripe session to credit allocation. Enables purchase history page and customer support for payment issues.

databaseSchemaChanges.schema:
model ApiKey {
  id         String   @id @default(cuid())
  userId     String
  name       String
  key        String   @unique
  lastUsedAt DateTime?
  expiresAt  DateTime?
  isActive   Boolean  @default(true)
  createdAt  DateTime @default(now())
  
  user User @relation(fields: [userId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  
  @@index([key])
  @@index([userId])
}
databaseSchemaChanges.reasoning: Future-proofing for API access. Enables mobile apps or third-party integrations to authenticate without credentials. Rate limiting can be applied per API key.

databaseSchemaChanges.schema:
model AdminAction {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  adminId   String
  action    String
  targetId  String?
  targetType String?
  metadata  Json     @default("{}")
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  
  admin User @relation(fields: [adminId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  
  @@index([adminId, createdAt])
  @@index([targetId])
}
databaseSchemaChanges.reasoning: Audit trail for admin actions (user bans, trainer verification, refunds). Critical for compliance and investigating abuse. Tracks who did what and when.

apiEnhancements.purpose: Proxy video streaming through API to avoid presigned URL expiration during long viewing sessions. Regenerates presigned URL on each chunk request.
apiEnhancements.responseSchema: Video stream with Content-Range headers, partial content 206 response
apiEnhancements.authentication: Required - JWT token in Authorization header or session cookie
apiEnhancements.rateLimit: 100 requests per minute per user (prevents abuse while allowing smooth playback)
apiEnhancements.caching: No caching - always stream fresh from S3 with regenerated presigned URLs

apiEnhancements.purpose: Versioned trainer listing endpoint with pagination, filtering, and sorting. Replaces unversioned /api/trainers.
apiEnhancements.requestSchema: { cursor?: string, limit?: number (default 30, max 100), sort?: 'rating' | 'reviews' | 'recent', specialty?: string, minRating?: number, verified?: boolean }
apiEnhancements.responseSchema: { trainers: TrainerWithUser[], nextCursor: string | null, hasMore: boolean, total: number }
apiEnhancements.authentication: Optional - returns more details if authenticated
apiEnhancements.rateLimit: 60 requests per minute for unauthenticated, 120 for authenticated
apiEnhancements.caching: Redis cache with 5-minute TTL, invalidate on trainer profile updates

apiEnhancements.purpose: Versioned video listing with infinite scroll pagination for user dashboard
apiEnhancements.requestSchema: { cursor?: string, limit?: number (default 20, max 50), status?: VideoStatus, workoutType?: string }
apiEnhancements.responseSchema: { videos: Video[], nextCursor: string | null, hasMore: boolean }
apiEnhancements.authentication: Required - returns only user's own videos
apiEnhancements.caching: No caching - always return fresh data for user's own content

apiEnhancements.purpose: Add timestamped comment to feedback. Used by trainers during video review.
apiEnhancements.responseSchema: { comment: Comment, totalComments: number }
apiEnhancements.authentication: Required - must be assigned trainer for this feedback
apiEnhancements.rateLimit: 60 requests per minute (allows rapid comment creation during review)
apiEnhancements.caching: Invalidate feedback cache on comment creation

apiEnhancements.purpose: Delete timestamped comment. Only before feedback submission.
apiEnhancements.responseSchema: { success: boolean, remainingComments: number }
apiEnhancements.authentication: Required - must be comment author and feedback must be IN_PROGRESS
apiEnhancements.caching: Invalidate feedback cache on comment deletion

apiEnhancements.purpose: Create Stripe Checkout session for credit package purchase
apiEnhancements.requestSchema: { packageId: string, successUrl?: string, cancelUrl?: string }
apiEnhancements.responseSchema: { sessionId: string, url: string }
apiEnhancements.rateLimit: 10 requests per 5 minutes per user (prevent checkout spam)

apiEnhancements.purpose: Retrieve user's purchase history with pagination
apiEnhancements.responseSchema: { purchases: Purchase[], nextCursor: string | null, hasMore: boolean, totalSpent: number }
apiEnhancements.caching: Redis cache with 2-minute TTL per user

apiEnhancements.purpose: Send email verification link to user's email address
apiEnhancements.responseSchema: { success: boolean, message: string, expiresIn: number }
apiEnhancements.authentication: Optional - can verify during or after registration
apiEnhancements.rateLimit: 3 requests per hour per email (prevent email spam)

apiEnhancements.purpose: Verify email address using token from email
apiEnhancements.responseSchema: { success: boolean, message: string, redirect?: string }
apiEnhancements.authentication: No auth required - token serves as authentication
apiEnhancements.rateLimit: 10 requests per minute per IP (prevent brute force token guessing)

apiEnhancements.purpose: Send password reset email with time-limited token
apiEnhancements.responseSchema: { success: boolean, message: string }

apiEnhancements.purpose: Reset password using token from email
apiEnhancements.requestSchema: { token: string, newPassword: string (min 8 chars, complexity rules) }
apiEnhancements.responseSchema: { success: boolean, message: string }
apiEnhancements.authentication: No auth required - token serves as authentication

apiEnhancements.purpose: Retrieve user's notifications with pagination and filtering
apiEnhancements.requestSchema: { cursor?: string, limit?: number (default 20, max 50), unreadOnly?: boolean }
apiEnhancements.responseSchema: { notifications: Notification[], nextCursor: string | null, unreadCount: number }

apiEnhancements.purpose: Mark notification as read
apiEnhancements.responseSchema: { success: boolean, notification: Notification }

apiEnhancements.purpose: Mark all user's notifications as read
apiEnhancements.responseSchema: { success: boolean, markedCount: number }

apiEnhancements.purpose: Poll for new notifications count (used by frontend polling)
apiEnhancements.responseSchema: { unreadCount: number, hasNew: boolean, latestNotification?: Notification }

apiEnhancements.purpose: Update user profile fields (name, fitness level, goals)
apiEnhancements.requestSchema: { name?: string, fitnessLevel?: FitnessLevel, goals?: string }
apiEnhancements.responseSchema: { user: User, updated: string[] }

apiEnhancements.purpose: Initiate email change with verification to new address
apiEnhancements.responseSchema: { success: boolean, message: string, verificationSent: boolean }

apiEnhancements.purpose: Change password with current password verification
apiEnhancements.responseSchema: { success: boolean, message: string }

apiEnhancements.purpose: Update trainer-specific profile fields
apiEnhancements.requestSchema: { bio?: string, specialties?: string[], hourlyRate?: number, availableHours?: object, maxDailyReviews?: number }
apiEnhancements.responseSchema: { trainer: Trainer, updated: string[] }
apiEnhancements.caching: Invalidate trainer cache and listings cache on update

apiEnhancements.purpose: Submit review and rating for trainer after receiving feedback
apiEnhancements.requestSchema: { feedbackId: string, rating: number (1-5), comment?: string }
apiEnhancements.responseSchema: { review: TrainerReview, trainerNewRating: number }
apiEnhancements.authentication: Required - must have received feedback from this trainer
apiEnhancements.caching: Invalidate trainer cache on review submission

apiEnhancements.purpose: Get paginated reviews for a trainer
apiEnhancements.responseSchema: { reviews: TrainerReview[], nextCursor: string | null, averageRating: number, totalReviews: number }

apiEnhancements.purpose: Admin endpoint to list and search users with filters
apiEnhancements.requestSchema: { cursor?: string, limit?: number, role?: Role, search?: string, emailVerified?: boolean }
apiEnhancements.responseSchema: { users: User[], nextCursor: string | null, total: number }
apiEnhancements.caching: No caching - admin data should be real-time

apiEnhancements.purpose: Admin endpoint to verify trainer certification
apiEnhancements.responseSchema: { trainer: Trainer, action: AdminAction }

apiEnhancements.purpose: Admin dashboard analytics endpoint
apiEnhancements.responseSchema: { totalUsers: number, totalTrainers: number, totalRevenue: number, activeVideos: number, completedFeedback: number, chartData: object }

caching.service: Upstash Redis (serverless Redis optimized for Next.js and Vercel)
useCases: Rate limiting counters with sliding window
caching.implementation: Install @upstash/redis and @upstash/ratelimit. Create /lib/redis.ts with singleton client. Create /lib/cache.ts with helper functions: get, set, del, invalidate. Implement cache-aside pattern: check cache, if miss fetch from DB and populate cache. Use Redis SETEX for automatic TTL. For rate limiting, use @upstash/ratelimit with sliding window algorithm. Environment variables: UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL, UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN.

queueing.service: Inngest (serverless background jobs and workflows) or BullMQ with Redis
useCases: Video processing: thumbnail generation, compression, format conversion
Email sending: verification emails, password resets, feedback notifications
Webhook retries: Stripe webhook failures with exponential backoff
Cleanup jobs: abandoned S3 uploads, expired tokens
Notification dispatching: batch processing of notifications
queueing.implementation: Recommended: Inngest for serverless simplicity. Install inngest and @inngest/next. Create /inngest/functions/ directory for job definitions. Define functions for: processVideo, sendEmail, retryWebhook, cleanupUploads. Configure Inngest client in /lib/inngest.ts. Create API route /api/inngest for Inngest to call. For local development, run Inngest dev server. For BullMQ alternative: install bullmq, create /lib/queues.ts, define queues and workers, requires Redis instance.

monitoring.errorTracking: Sentry - Install @sentry/nextjs. Configure in sentry.client.config.ts and sentry.server.config.ts. Capture unhandled errors, API errors, and custom events. Add user context (ID, email) to error reports. Set up source maps for production debugging.
monitoring.analytics: PostHog (open source, self-hosted option available) - Install posthog-js. Track custom events: video_uploaded, feedback_received, credit_purchased, trainer_selected. Track page views and user flows. Create funnels for conversion tracking. Privacy-friendly alternative to Google Analytics.
monitoring.apm: Vercel Analytics + Vercel Speed Insights for Next.js-specific metrics. Install @vercel/analytics and @vercel/speed-insights. Tracks Core Web Vitals, page load times, API response times. For self-hosted: OpenTelemetry with Jaeger or New Relic.
monitoring.implementation: Phase 1: Sentry for error tracking (critical). Phase 2: PostHog for product analytics. Phase 3: APM for performance monitoring. Create /lib/monitoring.ts with wrapper functions for consistent tracking. Add monitoring middleware to capture API performance. Set up alerts for error rate spikes and slow API responses.

cdn.service: Cloudflare CDN (free tier available) or Vercel Edge Network
cdn.implementation: For Vercel deployment: automatic Edge Network for static assets. Add Cache-Control headers to static files. For videos: consider Cloudflare R2 + CDN instead of S3 for lower bandwidth costs. Configure in next.config.js headers with appropriate cache durations. Use Image Optimization API for on-demand image resizing.

search.service: PostgreSQL Full-Text Search (built-in) initially, upgrade to Algolia or Meilisearch for advanced search
useCases: Trainer search by name, specialties, bio
Video search by title, description, workout type
Admin user search by name, email
search.implementation: Phase 1: Use Prisma full-text search with PostgreSQL tsvector. Add computed columns for search vectors. Phase 2: For better UX, integrate Meilisearch (open source, fast). Install meilisearch-js, set up index sync on data changes, implement search API endpoints. Meilisearch provides typo tolerance, faceted search, and instant results.

fileStorage.service: AWS S3 for primary storage, consider Cloudflare R2 for cost optimization
fileStorage.implementation: Current S3 setup is good. Enhancements: (1) Implement lifecycle policies to archive old videos to S3 Glacier after 1 year, (2) Add S3 bucket versioning for deletion protection, (3) Configure CloudFront as CDN for S3 content, (4) Implement multipart upload for files >100MB, (5) Add cleanup job for abandoned uploads (presigned URL generated but no DB record). For cost savings: migrate to Cloudflare R2 (S3-compatible, free egress bandwidth).

securityEnhancements.currentState: Zod is installed but not consistently used. Many API routes accept input without validation.
securityEnhancements.enhancement: Create comprehensive Zod schemas for all API inputs in /lib/validations.ts. Validate all request bodies, query params, and file uploads.
securityEnhancements.implementation: Define schemas for: CreateVideoSchema, CreateFeedbackSchema, CreateCommentSchema, UpdateProfileSchema, LoginSchema, RegisterSchema. Create middleware /lib/middleware/validate.ts that wraps route handlers and validates against schema. Return 400 with detailed validation errors. Example: const CreateVideoSchema = z.object({ title: z.string().min(3).max(100), description: z.string().max(500).optional(), workoutType: z.enum(['strength', 'cardio', 'flexibility']), fileSize: z.number().max(500_000_000) })

securityEnhancements.currentState: No rate limiting implemented. All endpoints are vulnerable to abuse and DDoS attacks.
securityEnhancements.enhancement: Implement sliding window rate limiting using @upstash/ratelimit with different limits per endpoint type.
securityEnhancements.implementation: Create /lib/middleware/rateLimit.ts with presets: authLimit (5 req/15min), apiLimit (120 req/min), publicLimit (60 req/min), uploadLimit (10 req/hour). Use Upstash Redis for distributed rate limiting (works across serverless instances). Apply middleware to route handlers: export const POST = rateLimit(authLimit, async (req) => {...}). Return 429 with Retry-After header. Track rate limits per user ID for authenticated, per IP for unauthenticated.

securityEnhancements.currentState: Passwords are hashed with bcrypt (good), but no account lockout, no 2FA, weak password requirements.
securityEnhancements.enhancement: Add failed login tracking, account lockout, password complexity requirements, optional 2FA with TOTP.
securityEnhancements.implementation: Add loginAttempts and lockedUntil fields to User model. Track failed logins in database. Lock account for 1 hour after 5 failed attempts. Require passwords: min 8 chars, 1 uppercase, 1 lowercase, 1 number, 1 special char. Use Zod schema with regex validation. For 2FA: add totpSecret field to User model, use speakeasy library to generate/verify TOTP codes, create /api/auth/2fa/setup and /api/auth/2fa/verify endpoints. Make 2FA optional but encourage during signup.

securityEnhancements.currentState: Relies on NextAuth.js default CSRF protection only. API routes have no additional CSRF tokens.
securityEnhancements.enhancement: Implement double-submit cookie pattern for all state-changing API routes.
securityEnhancements.implementation: Use Next.js middleware to set CSRF token cookie on all GET requests. Require CSRF token in X-CSRF-Token header for POST/PATCH/DELETE requests. Validate token match in middleware. Edge function middleware: export function middleware(req) { if (req.method !== 'GET') { const cookieToken = req.cookies.get('csrf-token'); const headerToken = req.headers.get('x-csrf-token'); if (cookieToken !== headerToken) return new Response('CSRF validation failed', { status: 403 }); } }. Exempt webhook endpoints that validate signatures differently.

securityEnhancements.currentState: User-generated content (feedback, comments) is not sanitized. React escapes by default but JSON injection is possible.
securityEnhancements.enhancement: Sanitize all HTML content server-side before storage using DOMPurify or isomorphic-dompurify.
securityEnhancements.implementation: Install isomorphic-dompurify. Create /lib/sanitize.ts utility: export const sanitizeHtml = (dirty: string) => DOMPurify.sanitize(dirty, { ALLOWED_TAGS: ['b', 'i', 'em', 'strong', 'p', 'br'], ALLOWED_ATTR: [] }). Apply to all user content before DB insert: const sanitized = sanitizeHtml(feedback.content). For plain text content, use DOMPurify with ALLOWED_TAGS: [] to strip all HTML.

securityEnhancements.currentState: Prisma ORM provides protection via parameterized queries, but raw SQL queries would be vulnerable.
securityEnhancements.enhancement: Audit all database queries to ensure no raw SQL. Add linting rules to prevent unsafe Prisma.$queryRaw usage.
securityEnhancements.implementation: ESLint rule to flag Prisma.$queryRaw. If raw queries are necessary, always use parameterized queries: prisma.$queryRaw`SELECT * FROM User WHERE id = ${userId}` (uses template literal for safe interpolation). Never concatenate user input: ❌ prisma.$queryRawUnsafe(`SELECT * FROM User WHERE id = '${userId}'`). Add database query logging in development to review all queries.

securityEnhancements.currentState: File type validation is client-side only. No file size enforcement server-side. No virus scanning.
securityEnhancements.enhancement: Server-side file validation using magic numbers, strict size limits, consider virus scanning for production.
securityEnhancements.implementation: Install file-type package. In upload API route, validate file type by reading magic numbers (first bytes): const fileTypeResult = await fileType.fromBuffer(buffer); if (!['video/mp4', 'video/quicktime'].includes(fileTypeResult.mime)) throw error. Enforce size limit: if (fileSize > 500_000_000) throw error. For production: integrate ClamAV or CloudMQ for virus scanning before accepting uploads. Quarantine suspicious files.

securityEnhancements.currentState: Environment variables in .env file. No validation. Some have empty string fallbacks (dangerous).
securityEnhancements.enhancement: Remove all fallbacks, validate required secrets on startup, consider HashiCorp Vault or AWS Secrets Manager for production.
securityEnhancements.implementation: Create /lib/env.ts with Zod schema: const envSchema = z.object({ DATABASE_URL: z.string().url(), NEXTAUTH_SECRET: z.string().min(32), STRIPE_SECRET_KEY: z.string().startsWith('sk_'), STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET: z.string().startsWith('whsec_'), AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID: z.string().min(16), AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY: z.string().min(40) }). Call envSchema.parse(process.env) in root layout and middleware. App crashes with clear error if any secret is missing. For production: rotate secrets regularly, use AWS Secrets Manager with automatic rotation.

securityEnhancements.currentState: No custom security headers configured. Missing CSP, X-Frame-Options, HSTS, etc.
securityEnhancements.enhancement: Implement comprehensive security headers in Next.js middleware or next.config.js.
securityEnhancements.implementation: Add to next.config.js headers: Content-Security-Policy (restrict script sources to same-origin and trusted CDNs), X-Frame-Options: DENY (prevent clickjacking), X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin, Permissions-Policy (restrict camera, microphone, geolocation), Strict-Transport-Security: max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains (enforce HTTPS). Example CSP: "default-src 'self'; script-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' https://js.stripe.com; connect-src 'self' https://api.stripe.com; frame-src https://js.stripe.com; img-src 'self' data: https:; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline';"

securityEnhancements.currentState: JWT sessions with NextAuth.js. No session rotation, no refresh tokens, long expiry.
securityEnhancements.enhancement: Implement session refresh tokens, rotate session IDs on privilege changes, add session timeout.
securityEnhancements.implementation: Configure NextAuth.js with shorter JWT expiry (1 hour instead of 30 days). Implement refresh token mechanism: store refreshToken in database Session model, when JWT expires frontend requests new JWT using refresh token. Rotate refresh token on each use (one-time use). Add sessionVersion field to User model, increment on password change or role change, invalidate all sessions when version doesn't match. Add idle timeout: track lastActivity in session, expire if no activity for 30 minutes.

securityEnhancements.currentState: Some API routes check authentication but don't verify user owns the resource being accessed.
securityEnhancements.enhancement: Implement resource-based authorization checks in all routes. Ensure users can only access their own data.
securityEnhancements.implementation: Create /lib/authorization.ts with helpers: canAccessVideo(userId, videoId), canProvideFeedback(trainerId, videoId), canModifyUser(requesterId, targetUserId, role). Use in route handlers before any operations: const video = await prisma.video.findUnique({ where: { id } }); if (video.userId !== session.user.id && session.user.role !== 'ADMIN') return new Response('Forbidden', { status: 403 }). Create reusable middleware: withAuth, withRole(['TRAINER', 'ADMIN']), withResourceAccess.

securityEnhancements.currentState: Stripe webhook signature verification has unsafe fallback to empty string.
securityEnhancements.enhancement: Remove fallback, enforce signature validation, add replay protection, log all webhooks.
securityEnhancements.implementation: Update webhook route: const sig = req.headers.get('stripe-signature'); if (!sig || !process.env.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET) return new Response('Webhook secret not configured', { status: 500 }). Verify signature: const event = stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(body, sig, process.env.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET). Add idempotency check using StripeWebhookLog.eventId before processing. Implement at-least-once processing: transaction wrapper ensures credit allocation and log insertion are atomic. Add monitoring for webhook failures.

performanceOptimizations.issue: N+1 query problems, missing indexes, over-fetching with select *, inefficient joins
performanceOptimizations.solution: Add composite indexes on frequently filtered columns, use Prisma select() to fetch only needed fields, implement includes with nested selects, use connection pooling
performanceOptimizations.expectedImprovement: 50-70% reduction in query execution time, 40% reduction in database load
performanceOptimizations.implementation: Add indexes: CREATE INDEX idx_video_user_created ON Video(userId, createdAt DESC); CREATE INDEX idx_feedback_trainer_status ON Feedback(trainerId, status); CREATE INDEX idx_trainer_rating_active ON Trainer(rating DESC, isActive). Update queries: prisma.video.findMany({ where: { userId }, select: { id: true, title: true, createdAt: true, status: true }, take: 20, orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' } }). Use include for relations instead of separate queries: prisma.feedback.findMany({ include: { trainer: { select: { user: { select: { name: true } }, rating: true } } } }). Enable Prisma connection pooling via pgBouncer or Supabase Pooler.

performanceOptimizations.issue: No caching layer. Every request hits database even for static data like trainer listings.
performanceOptimizations.solution: Implement Redis caching with appropriate TTLs, use cache-aside pattern, invalidate on writes
performanceOptimizations.expectedImprovement: 80% reduction in database queries for cached endpoints, 60% faster response times for trainer listings
performanceOptimizations.implementation: Install Upstash Redis. Create cache wrapper: async function getCached<T>(key: string, fetcher: () => Promise<T>, ttl: number): Promise<T> { const cached = await redis.get(key); if (cached) return JSON.parse(cached); const fresh = await fetcher(); await redis.setex(key, ttl, JSON.stringify(fresh)); return fresh; }. Use in trainer listing: const trainers = await getCached('trainers:all', () => prisma.trainer.findMany({...}), 300). Invalidate on updates: await redis.del('trainers:all') in trainer update route.

performanceOptimizations.issue: next/image not used anywhere. Trainer avatars and thumbnails not optimized.
performanceOptimizations.solution: Use next/image for all images, implement automatic WebP conversion, lazy loading, responsive sizes
performanceOptimizations.expectedImprovement: 70% reduction in image payload size, faster page loads, improved Lighthouse scores
performanceOptimizations.implementation: Replace tags with from next/image. Configure next.config.js images: { domains: ['your-s3-bucket.s3.amazonaws.com'], formats: ['image/webp', 'image/avif'], deviceSizes: [640, 750, 828, 1080, 1200, 1920], imageSizes: [16, 32, 48, 64, 96, 128, 256, 384] }. Use sizes prop for responsive images: . Enable lazy loading (default in next/image).

performanceOptimizations.issue: No manual code splitting. Large components loaded on every page. Heavy libraries not lazy-loaded.
performanceOptimizations.solution: Lazy load heavy components (VideoPlayer, UploadForm), implement route-based splitting, dynamic imports for libraries
performanceOptimizations.expectedImprovement: 40% reduction in initial bundle size, 30% faster Time to Interactive
performanceOptimizations.implementation: Use next/dynamic for heavy components: const VideoPlayer = dynamic(() => import('@/components/VideoPlayer'), { loading: () => <Skeleton />, ssr: false }). Lazy load libraries: const stripe = await import('stripe').then(m => new m.default(...)). Route-based splitting is automatic in Next.js App Router. Analyze bundle: npm install @next/bundle-analyzer, add to next.config.js, run ANALYZE=true npm run build to identify large dependencies.

performanceOptimizations.issue: No response compression. Large JSON payloads for lists. Over-fetching data.
performanceOptimizations.solution: Enable Brotli/Gzip compression, implement field selection in API, paginate all lists
performanceOptimizations.expectedImprovement: 60% reduction in API response size, faster network transfer
performanceOptimizations.implementation: Next.js automatically compresses responses in production. For explicit control, add compression middleware. Implement GraphQL-style field selection: GET /api/videos?fields=id,title,createdAt. Update queries: const selectedFields = req.query.fields?.split(',').reduce((acc, field) => ({ ...acc, [field]: true }), {}) || defaultFields; return prisma.video.findMany({ select: selectedFields }). Always paginate: never return unbounded lists.

performanceOptimizations.issue: Prisma client created without pooling optimization. Connections exhausted under load.
performanceOptimizations.solution: Configure Prisma connection pooling, use PgBouncer for connection management, optimize pool size
performanceOptimizations.expectedImprovement: Handle 5x more concurrent requests, prevent connection exhaustion errors
performanceOptimizations.implementation: Add to Prisma schema: datasource db { provider = 'postgresql', url = env('DATABASE_URL'), connection_limit = 10 }. For serverless: use Prisma Data Proxy or PgBouncer. Configure pool size based on (num_instances * connection_limit) < max_db_connections. For PostgreSQL: max_connections = 100, if 5 Next.js instances, set connection_limit = 15. Monitor connection usage: SELECT count(*) FROM pg_stat_activity.

performanceOptimizations.issue: All pages are dynamically rendered. Public pages (landing, pricing) could be static.
performanceOptimizations.solution: Use generateStaticParams for static pages, implement ISR for trainer listings, edge rendering for auth
performanceOptimizations.expectedImprovement: Instant page loads for static content, 90% reduction in server load for public pages
performanceOptimizations.implementation: Make landing page static: export const dynamic = 'force-static' in app/page.tsx. Use ISR for trainer listings: export const revalidate = 300 (revalidate every 5 minutes). Generate static params for trainer pages: export async function generateStaticParams() { const trainers = await prisma.trainer.findMany({ take: 50, orderBy: { rating: 'desc' } }); return trainers.map(t => ({ id: t.id })); }. Use Edge Runtime for auth middleware: export const config = { runtime: 'edge' }.

performanceOptimizations.issue: Large video files served through Next.js API route. No streaming optimization or CDN.
performanceOptimizations.solution: Implement proper video streaming with range requests, use CloudFront CDN, generate multiple quality versions
performanceOptimizations.expectedImprovement: 90% reduction in server bandwidth, instant video start, adaptive quality
performanceOptimizations.implementation: Update video streaming endpoint to support Range headers: const range = req.headers.get('range'); const videoStream = await s3.send(new GetObjectCommand({ Bucket, Key, Range: range })); return new Response(videoStream.Body, { status: 206, headers: { 'Content-Type': 'video/mp4', 'Content-Range': `bytes ${start}-${end}/${size}`, 'Accept-Ranges': 'bytes' } }). Configure CloudFront distribution for S3 bucket. Generate multiple quality versions (480p, 720p, 1080p) during processing.

performanceOptimizations.issue: Short staleTime (60s) causes frequent refetching. No query deduplication. Missing optimistic updates.
performanceOptimizations.solution: Optimize staleTime per query type, enable query deduplication, implement optimistic updates for mutations
performanceOptimizations.expectedImprovement: 40% reduction in unnecessary network requests, instant UI feedback for user actions
performanceOptimizations.implementation: Increase staleTime for stable data: useQuery({ queryKey: ['trainers'], queryFn: fetchTrainers, staleTime: 5 * 60 * 1000 }). Enable deduplication (default in React Query). Add optimistic updates: useMutation({ mutationFn: updateProfile, onMutate: async (newData) => { await queryClient.cancelQueries(['profile']); const previous = queryClient.getQueryData(['profile']); queryClient.setQueryData(['profile'], { ...previous, ...newData }); return { previous }; }, onError: (err, variables, context) => { queryClient.setQueryData(['profile'], context.previous); } }).

performanceOptimizations.issue: No prefetching of likely next pages. All components eagerly loaded. No intersection observer for below-fold content.
performanceOptimizations.solution: Prefetch trainer details on hover, lazy load below-fold components, implement virtual scrolling for long lists
performanceOptimizations.expectedImprovement: Instant navigation perception, 50% reduction in initial render time
performanceOptimizations.implementation: Prefetch on hover: <Link href='/trainers/123' onMouseEnter={() => queryClient.prefetchQuery(['trainer', '123'], fetchTrainer)}>. Lazy load below-fold: const BelowFoldContent = dynamic(() => import('./BelowFold')). Virtual scrolling for long lists: use @tanstack/react-virtual for video list with 1000+ items. Intersection observer for images: <Image loading='lazy' />.

thirdPartyIntegrations.purpose: Serverless Redis for caching and rate limiting
setupSteps: Sign up at upstash.com and create new Redis database (free tier: 10k commands/day)
Copy REST URL and token from database dashboard
Install packages: npm install @upstash/redis @upstash/ratelimit
Add to .env: UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL and UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN
Create /lib/redis.ts with client initialization
Create /lib/cache.ts with cache helper functions
Create /lib/middleware/rateLimit.ts with rate limiting middleware
Test locally with dev database, use production database for deployment
thirdPartyIntegrations.estimatedCost: $0-20/month (free tier covers most needs, paid tier $0.20 per 100k commands)

setupSteps: Sign up at sentry.io (free tier: 5k errors/month)
Create new Next.js project in Sentry dashboard
Run npx @sentry/wizard@latest -i nextjs
Configure error sampling rate in sentry configs (1.0 for dev, 0.1 for prod)
Add user context in NextAuth callback: Sentry.setUser({ id, email, role })
Test error tracking: throw new Error('Test Sentry') in dev
Set up alerts for error rate spikes in Sentry dashboard
thirdPartyIntegrations.estimatedCost: $0-26/month (free tier sufficient for MVP, Team plan $26/month for larger scale)

setupSteps: Sign up at posthog.com (free tier: 1M events/month)
Create new project and get API key
Install: npm install posthog-js
Create /lib/posthog.ts with client initialization
Add provider in app/layout.tsx to initialize on mount
Track custom events: posthog.capture('video_uploaded', { workoutType, duration })
Create funnels in PostHog dashboard: signup → upload → purchase
Set up session recording for debugging UX issues (opt-in only for privacy)
thirdPartyIntegrations.estimatedCost: $0-25/month (free tier sufficient for most, paid for high-volume)

setupSteps: Sign up at inngest.com (free tier: 1k function runs/month)
Install: npm install inngest @inngest/next
Create /inngest/client.ts with Inngest client
Create /inngest/functions/ directory for job definitions
Define functions: processVideo.ts, sendEmail.ts, cleanupUploads.ts
Create /app/api/inngest/route.ts to serve Inngest events
Deploy and register functions via Inngest dashboard
Test locally with Inngest dev server: npx inngest-cli dev
thirdPartyIntegrations.estimatedCost: $0-25/month (free tier for MVP, Pro plan $25/month for production)

thirdPartyIntegrations.purpose: S3-compatible storage with zero egress fees (cost optimization for video delivery)
setupSteps: Sign up at cloudflare.com and enable R2 (free tier: 10GB storage, 1M Class A ops/month)
Create R2 bucket via dashboard
Generate R2 API token with read/write permissions
S3 SDK is compatible: use @aws-sdk/client-s3 with R2 endpoint
Update /lib/s3.ts to use R2 endpoint: endpoint: 'https://ACCOUNT_ID.r2.cloudflarestorage.com'
Migrate existing S3 data using rclone or AWS CLI with custom endpoint
Configure custom domain for R2 bucket for cleaner URLs
thirdPartyIntegrations.estimatedCost: $0-15/month (free tier covers most, $0.015/GB/month storage, zero egress vs S3's $0.09/GB)

thirdPartyIntegrations.purpose: Modern email API (better developer experience than NodeMailer with SMTP)
setupSteps: Sign up at resend.com (free tier: 100 emails/day)
Verify domain ownership (add DNS records)
Get API key from dashboard
Install: npm install resend
Replace NodeMailer in /lib/email.ts with Resend client
Create email templates using React Email: npm install react-email @react-email/components
Design templates: VerificationEmail.tsx, PasswordResetEmail.tsx, FeedbackReadyEmail.tsx
Send emails: await resend.emails.send({ from: 'noreply@formfit.com', to, subject, react: <EmailTemplate /> })
thirdPartyIntegrations.estimatedCost: $0-20/month (free tier for MVP, Pro plan $20/month for 50k emails)

setupSteps: Connect GitHub repo to Vercel via vercel.com/new
Configure build settings (auto-detected for Next.js)
Add environment variables in Vercel dashboard (all from .env)
Enable Vercel Analytics: npm install @vercel/analytics
Enable Speed Insights: npm install @vercel/speed-insights
Configure custom domain via Vercel dashboard
Set up preview deployments for PRs (automatic)
Configure production environment protection (require approval for sensitive changes)
thirdPartyIntegrations.estimatedCost: $0-20/month (Hobby plan free for personal projects, Pro plan $20/month for commercial use with better limits)

unit.framework: Vitest (faster than Jest, better Next.js support)
coverage: Utility functions in /lib: validations.ts, auth.ts, email.ts, s3.ts, stripe.ts
unit.setup: Install vitest, @testing-library/react, @testing-library/jest-dom. Create vitest.config.ts with path aliases matching tsconfig. Write tests in /tests/unit/ or co-located *.test.ts files. Target 80%+ coverage for utility functions.

integration.framework: Vitest with MSW (Mock Service Worker) for API mocking
coverage: API routes: test request validation, authentication, authorization, response format
Database operations: test Prisma queries with test database
Webhook handlers: test Stripe webhook signature verification and processing
Authentication flows: registration, login, logout, session refresh
integration.setup: Install msw, @prisma/client for test DB. Create test database separate from development. Use MSW to mock external services (Stripe, S3, email). Write tests in /tests/integration/api/. Use beforeEach to seed test data and afterEach to cleanup.
integration.example: describe('POST /api/videos', () => { it('requires authentication', async () => { const res = await fetch('/api/videos', { method: 'POST' }); expect(res.status).toBe(401); }); });

e2e.framework: Playwright (better than Cypress for Next.js, supports multiple browsers)
coverage: Critical user flows: signup → verify email → login → upload video → select trainer → view feedback
Payment flow: purchase credits → Stripe checkout → webhook processing → credit allocation
Trainer flow: login as trainer → view assignments → provide feedback with timestamps → submit
Error scenarios: network failures, validation errors, payment failures
Mobile responsive testing on various viewports
e2e.setup: Install @playwright/test. Run npx playwright install to download browsers. Create /tests/e2e/ directory. Configure playwright.config.ts with baseURL, test database, headed mode for debugging. Use page object pattern for reusable components. Run tests in CI on every PR.
e2e.example: test('user can upload video', async ({ page }) => { await page.goto('/upload'); await page.fill('[name=title]', 'Test Video'); await page.setInputFiles('[type=file]', 'test-video.mp4'); await page.click('button[type=submit]'); await expect(page.locator('text=Upload successful')).toBeVisible(); });

breakdown.apiRoutes: 80%+ coverage (all happy paths and main error paths)
breakdown.components: 60%+ coverage (focus on logic, not just rendering)

coverage.tools: Vitest coverage with v8 provider. Generate reports: vitest --coverage. View HTML report in coverage/index.html. Add coverage thresholds in vitest.config.ts to prevent regressions.
coverage.enforcement: CI pipeline fails if coverage drops below thresholds. Require coverage report comment on PRs.

workflow: On PR: run linter (ESLint), type checking (tsc --noEmit), unit tests, integration tests
On merge to main: run all tests + E2E tests, build production bundle, deploy to Vercel preview
On release tag: deploy to Vercel production, run smoke tests against production
Nightly: run full E2E test suite, generate coverage reports, check for dependency vulnerabilities
cicd.setup: Create .github/workflows/test.yml. Use actions: checkout, setup-node, cache dependencies, run tests, upload coverage to Codecov. Create .github/workflows/deploy.yml for deployment. Add required status checks in GitHub repo settings to enforce passing tests before merge.
cicd.example: .github/workflows/test.yml: on: [pull_request] jobs: test: runs-on: ubuntu-latest steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v3 - uses: actions/setup-node@v3 with: { node-version: '18', cache: 'npm' } - run: npm ci - run: npm run lint - run: npm run type-check - run: npm run test:unit - run: npm run test:integration

apiVersioning.strategy: URL path versioning (e.g., /api/v1/videos) for clear version indication and easy routing
apiVersioning.implementation: Create /app/api/v1/ directory structure. Move current routes to v1. Set up version detection middleware. Maintain backward compatibility for at least 6 months after deprecation notice. Use API version header as fallback: Accept-Version: v1.
apiVersioning.deprecationPolicy: Announce deprecation 3 months in advance via email to developers, add deprecation headers (Sunset: date), log deprecation warnings, maintain docs for legacy versions.
apiVersioning.reasoning: Prevents breaking changes from affecting existing clients. Enables gradual migration to new API versions. Industry standard practice.

logging.strategy: Structured JSON logging with correlation IDs for request tracing
logging.implementation: Create /lib/logger.ts using pino (fast, low-overhead). Log levels: error, warn, info, debug. Include: timestamp, level, message, userId, requestId, metadata. Example: logger.info({ userId, videoId, duration }, 'Video uploaded successfully'). Send logs to Axiom or Datadog for aggregation and search.
logging.retention: Keep logs for 30 days in development, 90 days in production for debugging and compliance

tracing.strategy: Distributed tracing with OpenTelemetry for understanding request flows across services
tracing.implementation: Install @opentelemetry/api, @opentelemetry/sdk-node, @opentelemetry/auto-instrumentations-node. Auto-instrument HTTP, database, and external service calls. Export traces to Jaeger (self-hosted) or Honeycomb (SaaS). Visualize slow requests and bottlenecks.
tracing.useCases: Identify slow database queries, debug webhook processing, track external API latency

metrics.strategy: Application metrics for capacity planning and alerting
metrics.implementation: Track: request rate, error rate, response time (p50, p95, p99), database query duration, cache hit rate, video upload success rate, payment conversion rate. Use Prometheus + Grafana or Vercel Analytics. Set up alerts: error rate > 5%, response time p95 > 2s, cache hit rate < 50%.
metrics.dashboards: Create dashboards: API health, user engagement, business metrics (revenue, signups, retention)

databaseMigrations.approach: Use Prisma Migrate for schema changes with careful rollout
steps: Create migration: npx prisma migrate dev --name add_email_verification
Review generated SQL in prisma/migrations/ before applying
Test migration on staging database with production data snapshot
Create rollback script for each migration (important for data changes)
Apply to production during low-traffic window with database backup
Monitor for errors and performance impact post-migration
Keep backward compatibility during migration (e.g., make new columns optional initially)
databaseMigrations.safeguards: Always backup database before migration. Use transactions for data migrations. Add new columns as optional, populate them, then make required. Never drop columns without deprecation period.

apiMigration.approach: Gradual migration to v1 APIs with dual support period
steps: Create v1 endpoints with new schema and validation
Update internal frontend to use v1 endpoints
Test thoroughly before deprecating unversioned endpoints
Add deprecation warnings to unversioned endpoints (Deprecation header)
Monitor usage of old endpoints via logging
Remove old endpoints after 6 months or when usage drops to <1%

dataBackfill.approach: Safe backfill scripts for populating new fields in existing records
steps: Write backfill script as Prisma transaction
Test on subset of data (100 records) first
Run backfill in batches (1000 records at a time) to avoid long transactions
Add progress logging and error handling
Make script idempotent (safe to run multiple times)
Example: Backfill User.emailVerified for existing users set to true
